004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.reflect.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
025
026/**
027 * Reference implementation of the public {@link TokenReader} surface for the CBOR format
028 * (RFC 8949).
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Backed by the existing internal {@link CborInputStream} plumbing.  CBOR's wire format is already
032 * token-shaped (major-type encoded data items), so this cursor maps almost directly onto
033 * {@link CborInputStream#readDataType()}.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
036 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
037 *    <li>The cursor is a true O(1)-memory streaming cursor ({@link #isStreaming()} == <jk>true</jk>).
038 *    <li>The cursor is purely structural; object swaps and {@code @Schema} annotations have no
039 *       effect.  Use the POJO databind path on {@link CborParser} for those.
040 * </ul>
041 */
042@SuppressWarnings({
043   "resource" // The cursor's underlying ParserPipe is owned by the caller via try-with-resources on the cursor itself; Eclipse JDT flags the inner pipe as unclosed but that's by design.
044})
045public class CborTokenReader implements TokenReader {
046
047   private static final long INDEFINITE = -1L;
048
049   /**
050    * Default maximum number of nested CBOR semantic tags (major type 6) the cursor will unwrap
051    * before throwing a {@link ParseException}.
052    *
053    * <p>
054    * CBOR allows a value to be wrapped in an arbitrary number of semantic tags
055    * (e.g. <c>0xC0 0xC0 ... value</c>).  Each tag is unwrapped recursively by {@link #next()}, so a
056    * pathologically deep chain of tags would otherwise blow the JVM call stack with a
057    * {@link StackOverflowError}.  This bound converts that failure mode into a clean, catchable
058    * {@link ParseException}.
059    */
060   static final int DEFAULT_MAX_TAG_NESTING_DEPTH = 64;
061
062   private final CborInputStream is;
063   private final ParserPipe pipe;
064   private final CborParserSession session;
065
066   private int maxTagNestingDepth = DEFAULT_MAX_TAG_NESTING_DEPTH;
067   // Current depth of the in-progress recursive tag unwrap (reset to 0 once a value is emitted).
068   private int tagNestingDepth;
069
070   private TokenType currentToken = TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE;
071   private boolean ended;
072
073   // Container stack: parallel arrays — kind (0=array, 1=map) + remaining element count
074   // (INDEFINITE for indefinite-length).  For maps the count covers key+value pairs at 2 each.
075   private long[] stackRemaining = new long[16];
076   private boolean[] stackIsMap = new boolean[16];
077   // Per-level: at map-level, true when next emit should be a FIELD_NAME (key); false when next
078   // emit should be a value.  Stored per-level so nested containers don't corrupt each other.
079   private boolean[] stackAwaitingKey = new boolean[16];
080   private int depth;
081
082   // Scratch state for current token's value.
083   private String currentString;
084   private String currentNumberLexeme;
085   private Number currentNumber;
086   private boolean currentBoolean;
087   private byte[] currentBinary;
088
089   // Binary-native opt-in state (175ad).  Defaults preserve the normalize-by-default contract:
090   // nativeMode == false ⇒ tag numbers are dropped, simple values surface as VALUE_NULL with no
091   // metadata.  When nativeMode is true, the TAG branch accumulates tag numbers into 'tags' as
092   // it recurses, the SIMPLE branch captures the simple int into 'simpleValue', and 'nativeKind'
093   // classifies the resulting token for format-agnostic consumers.
094   private boolean nativeMode;
095   private BinaryNativeKind nativeKind = BinaryNativeKind.NONE;
096   private long[] tags = new long[4];
097   private int tagCount;
098   private int simpleValue;
099
100   /**
101    * Constructor with default settings.
102    *
103    * @param pipe The parser input pipe.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
104    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying stream.
105    */
106   public CborTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException {
107      this(pipe, null);
108   }
109
110   /**
111    * Constructor used by {@link CborParserSession#parseTokens(Object)} to plumb the calling
112    * session through so that {@link #read(Class)} can delegate to the CBOR databind path.
113    *
114    * @param pipe The parser input pipe.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
115    * @param session The {@link CborParserSession} for {@link #read(Class)} delegation, or
116    *    <jk>null</jk> to disable {@code read}.
117    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying stream.
118    */
119   public CborTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe, CborParserSession session) throws IOException {
120      this.pipe = pipe;
121      this.is = new CborInputStream(pipe);
122      this.session = session;
123   }
124
125   /**
126    * Sets the maximum number of nested CBOR semantic tags this cursor will unwrap before throwing
127    * a {@link ParseException}.
128    *
129    * <p>
130    * Defaults to {@link #DEFAULT_MAX_TAG_NESTING_DEPTH} (64).  A chain of more than this many tags
131    * wrapping a single value causes {@link #next()} to throw rather than risk a
132    * {@link StackOverflowError}.
133    *
134    * @param value The new maximum nesting depth.  Must be {@code >= 0}.
135    * @return This object.
136    */
137   public CborTokenReader setMaxTagNestingDepth(int value) {
138      maxTagNestingDepth = value;
139      return this;
140   }
141
142   /**
143    * Enables or disables binary-native opt-in mode.
144    *
145    * <p>
146    * When enabled, CBOR semantic tags are captured on the tag stack rather than discarded
147    * (surface them via {@link #getTagCount()} / {@link #getTag(int)}), and CBOR simple values
148    * surface their int via {@link #getSimpleValue()}.  When disabled (default), tags are
149    * silently unwrapped and simple values collapse to {@link TokenType#VALUE_NULL} with no
150    * metadata &mdash; identical to the pre-native-mode behavior.
151    *
152    * @param value <jk>true</jk> to enable native mode.
153    * @return This object.
154    */
155   public CborTokenReader setNativeMode(boolean value) {
156      nativeMode = value;
157      return this;
158   }
159
160   // ==============================================================================================
161   // State-machine summary.  CBOR is already a token-shaped wire format (RFC 8949 major types), so
162   // next() reads the next data-type tag via CborInputStream.readDataType() and dispatches based
163   // on three things: (1) whether we're at end-of-input, (2) whether a definite-length container
164   // has exhausted its element count (emit END_*), or whether the data type is BREAK (also emit
165   // END_*), and (3) within a map, whether we're at a key position or value position.
166   //
167   // Per-level container state lives on parallel stacks (stackRemaining + stackIsMap +
168   // stackAwaitingKey, indexed by depth).  A stackRemaining value of -1 means indefinite-length
169   // in that case the next() loop relies on CborInputStream returning DataType.BREAK instead of
170   // counting elements.
171   // ==============================================================================================
172   @Override /* TokenReader */
173   @SuppressWarnings({
174      "java:S3776" // Token-reader state machine; complexity is inherent to the format decode loop.
175   })
176   public TokenType next() throws IOException, ParseException {
177      if (ended) {
178         currentToken = TokenType.END_OF_STREAM;
179         return currentToken;
180      }
181      // Reset scratch.
182      currentString = null;
183      currentNumberLexeme = null;
184      currentNumber = null;
185      currentBinary = null;
186      // Native-mode metadata: only reset at outermost entry — recursive tag unwraps preserve the
187      // accumulated tag stack so nested tag(tag(value)) round-trips losslessly.
188      if (tagNestingDepth == 0) {
189         nativeKind = BinaryNativeKind.NONE;
190         tagCount = 0;
191      }
192
193      // Inside a definite-length container at element-count zero: emit END_*.
194      if (depth > 0 && stackRemaining[depth - 1] == 0) {
195         popContainer();
196         return currentToken;
197      }
198
199      DataType dt;
200      try {
201         dt = is.readDataType();
202      } catch (IOException e) {
203         if (depth == 0) {
204            ended = true;
205            currentToken = TokenType.END_OF_STREAM;
206            return currentToken;
207         }
208         throw e;
209      }
210
211      // Inside an indefinite-length container, BREAK closes it.
212      if (dt == DataType.BREAK) {
213         if (depth == 0 || stackRemaining[depth - 1] != INDEFINITE)
214            throw new ParseException("Unexpected BREAK token outside indefinite-length container");
215         popContainer();
216         return currentToken;
217      }
218
219      // Map key position: CBOR keys can be any data type, but the token vocabulary expects
220      // FIELD_NAME with a string view.  Convert string keys directly; coerce other key types
221      // to their toString() form (matches the databind path's behavior).
222      if (depth > 0 && stackIsMap[depth - 1] && stackAwaitingKey[depth - 1]) {
223         currentToken = TokenType.FIELD_NAME;
224         currentString = readScalarAsString(dt);
225         consumedOneElement();  // flips awaiting-key: key->value, decrements remaining count
226         return currentToken;
227      }
228
229      // Value position (in array, in map after key, or at root).
230      switch (dt) {
231         case ARRAY -> {
232            pushContainer(false, is.readLength());
233            currentToken = TokenType.START_ARRAY;
234         }
235         case MAP -> {
236            var n = is.readLength();
237            // MAP length is pair-count; track it as 2*n element-counts.
238            pushContainer(true, n == INDEFINITE ? INDEFINITE : n * 2);
239            currentToken = TokenType.START_OBJECT;
240            // First emit inside the map will be a key; pushContainer already set this
241            // for map-level entries.
242         }
243         case UINT, NINT -> {
244            var v = is.readSignedLong();
245            currentNumber = v;
246            currentNumberLexeme = Long.toString(v);
247            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER;
248            consumedOneElement();
249         }
250         case FLOAT -> {
251            var v = is.readDouble();
252            currentNumber = v;
253            currentNumberLexeme = Double.toString(v);
254            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER;
255            consumedOneElement();
256         }
257         case BOOLEAN -> {
258            currentBoolean = is.readBoolean();
259            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN;
260            consumedOneElement();
261         }
262         case NULL, UNDEFINED -> {
263            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_NULL;
264            consumedOneElement();
265         }
266         case STRING -> {
267            currentString = is.readString();
268            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_STRING;
269            consumedOneElement();
270         }
271         case BINARY -> {
272            currentBinary = is.readBinary();
273            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_BINARY;
274            consumedOneElement();
275         }
276         case TAG, SIMPLE -> {
277            // Q3: by default normalize to common scalars.  TAG: skip the tag and read the
278            // next item as the wrapped value.  SIMPLE: emit as VALUE_NULL (best-effort).
279            // In native mode (175ad): TAG numbers accumulate on the tag stack across
280            // recursion; SIMPLE captures the simple int as metadata on the VALUE_NULL token.
281            if (dt == DataType.TAG) {
282               var n = is.readLength();
283               if (nativeMode) {
284                  if (tagCount == tags.length)
285                     tags = java.util.Arrays.copyOf(tags, tags.length * 2);
286                  tags[tagCount++] = n;
287                  nativeKind = BinaryNativeKind.CBOR_TAG;
288               }
289               // Guard against pathologically deep tag chains blowing the JVM stack.
290               if (tagNestingDepth >= maxTagNestingDepth)
291                  throw new ParseException(
292                     "CBOR tag nesting depth exceeded the maximum of {0}.  Increase the limit via CborTokenReader.setMaxTagNestingDepth(int) if this is intentional.",
293                     maxTagNestingDepth);
294               tagNestingDepth++;
295               try {
296                  return next();  // consume the wrapped value
297               } finally {
298                  tagNestingDepth--;
299               }
300            }
301            if (nativeMode) {
302               simpleValue = (int) is.readLength();
303               nativeKind = BinaryNativeKind.CBOR_SIMPLE;
304            }
305            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_NULL;
306            consumedOneElement();
307         }
308         default -> throw new ParseException("Unexpected CBOR data type: {0}", dt);
309      }
310      return currentToken;
311   }
312
313   private String readScalarAsString(DataType dt) throws IOException, ParseException {
314      switch (dt) {
315         case STRING:    return is.readString();
316         case UINT, NINT: return Long.toString(is.readSignedLong());
317         case BINARY: {
318            var b = is.readBinary();
319            return new String(b, java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
320         }
321         case BOOLEAN:   return Boolean.toString(is.readBoolean());
322         case FLOAT:     return Double.toString(is.readDouble());
323         default:
324            throw new ParseException("Cannot use CBOR data type {0} as a map key", dt);
325      }
326   }
327
328   private void pushContainer(boolean isMap, long remaining) {
329      if (depth == stackRemaining.length) {
330         var n = depth * 2;
331         var nr = new long[n];
332         var nm = new boolean[n];
333         var nk = new boolean[n];
334         System.arraycopy(stackRemaining, 0, nr, 0, depth);
335         System.arraycopy(stackIsMap, 0, nm, 0, depth);
336         System.arraycopy(stackAwaitingKey, 0, nk, 0, depth);
337         stackRemaining = nr;
338         stackIsMap = nm;
339         stackAwaitingKey = nk;
340      }
341      stackRemaining[depth] = remaining;
342      stackIsMap[depth] = isMap;
343      stackAwaitingKey[depth] = isMap;  // map starts awaiting a key
344      depth++;
345   }
346
347   private void popContainer() {
348      depth--;
349      currentToken = stackIsMap[depth] ? TokenType.END_OBJECT : TokenType.END_ARRAY;
350      // The closed container counted as one value-emit in the parent's context.
351      consumedOneElement();
352   }
353
354   private void consumedOneElement() {
355      if (depth == 0)
356         return;
357      var top = depth - 1;
358      if (stackRemaining[top] != INDEFINITE)
359         stackRemaining[top]--;
360      // In a map, alternate key/value positions per element.
361      if (stackIsMap[top])
362         stackAwaitingKey[top] = !stackAwaitingKey[top];
363   }
364
365   @Override /* TokenReader */
366   public TokenType getCurrentToken() {
367      return currentToken;
368   }
369
370   @Override /* TokenReader */
371   public int getDepth() {
372      return depth;
373   }
374
375   @Override /* TokenReader */
376   public String getFieldName() {
377      if (currentToken != TokenType.FIELD_NAME)
378         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not FIELD_NAME (was " + currentToken + ")");
379      return currentString;
380   }
381
382   @Override /* TokenReader */
383   public String getString() {
384      switch (currentToken) {
385         case VALUE_STRING:  return currentString;
386         case FIELD_NAME:    return currentString;
387         case VALUE_NUMBER:  return currentNumberLexeme;
388         case VALUE_BOOLEAN: return Boolean.toString(currentBoolean);
389         case VALUE_NULL:    return null;
390         default: throw new IllegalStateException(
391            "Current token does not have a string view (was " + currentToken + ")");
392      }
393   }
394
395   @Override /* TokenReader */
396   public Number getNumber() throws ParseException {
397      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER)
398         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not VALUE_NUMBER (was " + currentToken + ")");
399      return currentNumber;
400   }
401
402   @Override /* TokenReader */
403   public String getNumberLexeme() {
404      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER)
405         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not VALUE_NUMBER (was " + currentToken + ")");
406      return currentNumberLexeme;
407   }
408
409   @Override /* TokenReader */
410   public boolean getBool() {
411      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN)
412         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not VALUE_BOOLEAN (was " + currentToken + ")");
413      return currentBoolean;
414   }
415
416   @Override /* TokenReader */
417   public byte[] getBinary() {
418      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_BINARY)
419         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not VALUE_BINARY (was " + currentToken + ")");
420      return currentBinary;
421   }
422
423   @Override /* TokenReader */
424   public boolean canRead() throws IOException, ParseException {
425      // CBOR has no separators to advance through; the cursor is "at a value boundary" iff
426      // (a) we're not inside a definite-length container that's exhausted, and
427      // (b) inside an indefinite-length container, the next byte isn't BREAK, and
428      // (c) the stream isn't ended.
429      if (ended)
430         return false;
431      if (depth > 0) {
432         var remaining = stackRemaining[depth - 1];
433         if (remaining == 0)
434            return false;
435         if (remaining == INDEFINITE && is.peekBreak())
436            return false;
437      }
438      return true;
439   }
440
441   @Override /* TokenReader */
442   public <T> T read(Class<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
443      return read(session().getClassMeta(type));
444   }
445
446   @Override /* TokenReader */
447   public <T> T read(ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
448      var s = session();
449      if (!canRead())
450         throw new IllegalStateException(MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE);
451      try {
452         T o = s.parseAnything(type, is, null, null);
453         consumedOneElement();
454         currentToken = TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE;
455         return o;
456      } catch (Exception e) {
457         if (e instanceof IOException ioe) throw ioe;
458         if (e instanceof ParseException pe) throw pe;
459         throw new ParseException(s, "read failed: {0}", e.getMessage());
460      }
461   }
462
463   @Override /* TokenReader */
464   public <T> T read(Type type, Type... args) throws IOException, ParseException {
465      return read(session().<T>getClassMeta(type, args));
466   }
467
468   private CborParserSession session() {
469      if (session == null)
470         throw new UnsupportedOperationException(
471            "read is not available on this CborTokenReader (no session attached). " +
472            "Open the cursor via CborParser.parseTokens(...) to enable read.");
473      return session;
474   }
475
476   @Override /* TokenReader */
477   public void skipChildren() throws IOException, ParseException {
478      if (currentToken != TokenType.START_OBJECT && currentToken != TokenType.START_ARRAY)
479         return;
480      var startDepth = depth - 1;
481      while (depth > startDepth) {
482         var t = next();
483         if (t == TokenType.END_OF_STREAM)
484            throw new ParseException("Unexpected end of input while skipping children");
485      }
486   }
487
488   @Override /* TokenReader */
489   public boolean isStreaming() {
490      return true;
491   }
492
493   @Override /* TokenReader */
494   public void close() throws IOException {
495      pipe.close();
496   }
497
498   @Override /* TokenReader */
499   public BinaryNativeKind getNativeKind() {
500      return nativeKind;
501   }
502
503   @Override /* TokenReader */
504   public int getTagCount() {
505      return tagCount;
506   }
507
508   @Override /* TokenReader */
509   public long getTag(int index) {
510      if (index < 0 || index >= tagCount)
511         throw new IndexOutOfBoundsException("Tag index " + index + " out of bounds for tagCount " + tagCount);
512      return tags[index];
513   }
514
515   @Override /* TokenReader */
516   public int getExtType() {
517      throw new IllegalStateException("CBOR cursor has no MsgPack ext type.");
518   }
519
520   @Override /* TokenReader */
521   public int getSimpleValue() {
522      if (nativeKind != BinaryNativeKind.CBOR_SIMPLE)
523         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not a CBOR simple value (nativeKind=" + nativeKind + ")");
524      return simpleValue;
525   }
526}
